title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What Are The Documents Required For a Term Plan?
Term insurance is a type of life insurance you can purchase with affordable premiums. In case of your untimely demise, the death benefit can offer your family financial support.
But before purchasing a term plan, it is useful to understand the documents required for term insurance. Although each insurer may have different requirements, being aware of the documents in general can help you prepare ahead of time and may facilitate the term insurance application process.
Documents Required for Term Insurance
For purchasing a term insurance plan, keeping the following documents ready for you may ensure a quick and hassle-free purchase of the plan -
1. Proof of Identity Document (ID) and Address
For the Know Your Customer (KYC) process, you may need to submit a valid address and ID proof. The documents verifying the same usually include-
Aadhaar Card
Passport
Driving License
Voters ID
Job card issued by NREGA duly signed by an officer of the State Government,
The letter issued by the National Population Register containing details of name, address
2. Age Proof
Usually, individuals between the age of 18 years to 65 years may be eligible to purchase a term insurance plan. Hence, one of the documents required for term insurance can be the age proof. You may use your PAN Card or Aadhaar Card as proof of the same. If you are planning to buy a term plan online, you may scan or click a photo of both the front and back of the Aadhar Card. If you are submitting your PAN card, only the front of the card may be acceptable.
3. Income Proof
When applying for a term insurance plan, submitting documents required for term insurance may include providing evidence of income. Depending on your profession or source of income, there are different types of proof of income. These documents give insurers a sense of your ability to pay for the premiums and help them choose an appropriate coverage amount.
For Salaried Individuals
These documents can confirm your regular income and its stability.
Recent salary slips.
Bank statements to show salary deposits.
Form 16.
Income Tax Returns (ITRs) for the last 3 years.
For Self-Employed Professionals
Proof of income for term insurance can include the following if you are a freelancer or own your own business:
Profit and loss account of the business for the last 3 years.
Balance sheet for the last 3 years.
Income Tax Returns (ITRs) for the last 3 years.
Bank statements to show business dealings.
GST returns (if applicable).
For NRIs
Non-resident Indians may need:
Regular deposit bank statements.
Foreign employment income statements.
Tax documents from the residence country.
Do note that the specific documents required for a term plan may differ by insurer.
4. Medical reports
When buying term insurance, you may be required to fill in details about your pre-existing health conditions. Medical tests may be needed by the insurer to better understand your situation while providing you with a life cover. Once you have submitted your medical reports, your life insurance provider may quote you the premium for your term plan.
5. Other Mandatory Documents
PAN Card or Form 60
Recent passport size Photograph
Why are Documents Required to Buy Term Insurance?
When buying a term insurance plan, submitting the documents required for term insurance is essential. These documents serve multiple purposes:
Help confirms your resident status and personal details.
Provides real-time information about your health for accurate risk assessment.
Speeds up the processing and approval of your policy.
Enables quicker payout to nominees in case of the life assured’s death.
If the policy lapses, the submitted documents make the renewal process easier.
List of Documents Needed to File a Term Insurance Claim
When making a claim, submitting the right documents required for term insurance may help speed up the process. Commonly requested documents include:
Death certificate of the insured.
Original term insurance policy document.
Identity proof of nominee.
Claim form signed by the nominee.
Bank account information for the nominee.
Medical records, if needed.
If the death was not intentional, file a FIR or an accident report.
Requirements can differ between insurers, so checking with the company beforehand may be useful.
What to Do if You Lose or Misplace Original Documents Needed for Term Insurance?
If original documents are lost, it may be possible to provide alternatives. Points to consider:
Request a duplicate policy document from the insurer
Use notarized copies of identity and address proofs
Submit a written declaration explaining the loss
Keep digital copies of all documents for future reference
Contact customer support for guidance
Taking these steps may reduce delays and complications during policy use or claim settlement.
FAQs
Which income proof is required for term insurance?
Income proof may vary based on occupation and insurance policies. Salaried individuals need to submit salary slips or Form 16, self-employed applicants may provide ITRs, and NRIs may show overseas bank statements or tax documents.
How can I submit the documents required for term insurance purchase?
Documents may be submitted online using the insurer's site, by email, or in person at the branch. Scanned copies or images are also acceptable for expedited processing.
How many documents are required for a term insurance claim?
The papers required vary depending on the insurer and the type of policy. Typically, it comprises the policy, identification documents, claim form, death certificate and related medical reports.
